channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'The Wire' actor Clarke Peters explains why he couldn't say no to 'The Boroughs'
item.description: "He's the guy I want to be when I grow up," Peters says of his Wire character, Lester Freamon. In The Boroughs, Peters plays a member of a retirement community that's plagued by mysterious forces.
item.content:encoded: "He's the guy I want to be when I grow up," Peters says of his Wire character, Lester Freamon. In The Boroughs, Peters plays a member of a retirement community that's plagued by mysterious forces.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: A new 'Cape Fear' remake rolls out one surprise after another
item.description: Javier Bardem is riveting in this 10-part Apple TV miniseries about a man who, recently released from prison, goes on to terrorize his former attorney.
item.content:encoded: Javier Bardem is riveting in this 10-part Apple TV miniseries about a man who, recently released from prison, goes on to terrorize his former attorney.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: Marjane Satrapi, author of 'Persepolis,' dies at 56
item.description: The Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker was perhaps most well-known for the graphic memoir, and subsequent film, about her life during the Iranian revolution in 1979.
item.content:encoded:
The Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker was perhaps most well-known for the graphic memoir, and subsequent film, about her life during the Iranian revolution in 1979.

(Image credit: Ernesto S. Ruscio/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck

item.title: Breaking News from Wait Wait...Don't Tell Me: Our new Judge and Scorekeeper is announced!
item.description: The orange smoke has risen from the chimney of The Studebaker Theater, which can only mean one thing: We've named a new official Judge and Scorekeeper!
item.content:encoded:
The orange smoke has risen from the chimney of The Studebaker Theater, which can only mean one thing: We've named a new official Judge and Scorekeeper!

(Image credit: Eric Michael Peterson)

item.title: How having zero points in tennis — or 'love' — came to sound so sweet
item.description: There are theories that "love" in the tennis context has French, English or Dutch origins. But like many words, historians and language experts say it's hard to pin down the "right" answer.
item.content:encoded:
There are theories that "love" in the tennis context has French, English or Dutch origins. But like many words, historians and language experts say it's hard to pin down the "right" answer.

(Image credit: Kirsty Wigglesworth)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: 'The ship of my life': Sting talks the comeback of his musical
item.description: The Last Ship, the first musical to be staged at New York's Metropolitan Opera, inspired by Sting's childhood growing up in the shadow of a shipyard.
item.content:encoded:
The Last Ship, the first musical to be staged at New York's Metropolitan Opera, inspired by Sting's childhood growing up in the shadow of a shipyard.

(Image credit: Mark Senior)
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Critics say adding Trump's arch to D.C. would take something away: the view
item.description: A second federal agency advanced Trump's arch proposal on Thursday, but requested more information about its implications for everything from the landscape to pedestrian safety to aviation.
item.content:encoded: A second federal agency advanced Trump's arch proposal on Thursday, but requested more information about its implications for everything from the landscape to pedestrian safety to aviation.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Firings at CBS' '60 Minutes' reflect the fight for media control in the age of Trump
item.description: The show's new leader says he fired star Scott Pelley for insubordination. Pelley says he was defending the integrity of the show's journalism after three top executives and two reporters were fired.
item.content:encoded:
The show's new leader says he fired star Scott Pelley for insubordination. Pelley says he was defending the integrity of the show's journalism after three top executives and two reporters were fired.

(Image credit: CBS Photo Archive/CBS via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Tribeca Film Festival opens, 25 years after helping New York recover from 9/11
item.description: Actor Robert De Niro and producing partner Jane Rosenthal created the festival in the wake of the terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center.
item.content:encoded:
Actor Robert De Niro and producing partner Jane Rosenthal created the festival in the wake of the terrorist attacks on the World Trade Center.

(Image credit: Michael Ochs Archives/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Sinister forces invade an upscale retirement community in 'The Boroughs'
item.description: The team behind Stranger Things has another series about a group of quirky misfits and mysterious creatures. The Boroughs is well worth seeing — as much for its veteran cast as for its story.
item.content:encoded: The team behind Stranger Things has another series about a group of quirky misfits and mysterious creatures. The Boroughs is well worth seeing — as much for its veteran cast as for its story.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli
